title: Intensely Tangy Ligurian Kippers Pasta
description: A tangy and chewy one-pot Italian dish featuring kippers, this Ligurian pasta is a Jain-friendly option, served on a wooden board. The combination of kippers, roasted vegetables, and tangy sauce creates a unique flavor profile.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the minced roasted garlic, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced roasted red peppers to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the lemon juice, white wine vinegar, salt, and black pepper, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the flaked kippers to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, breaking up with a spoon, until heated through.
Drain the cooked pasta and add it to the skillet, tossing to combine with the kipper mixture.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste, then stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the pasta hot on a wooden board, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the dish rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
